INDUSTRIAL DESIGNERS SOCIETY OF AMERICA

HOME - AWARDS & RECOGNITIONS

Awards & Recognitions

Highlighting the exceptional work of our design community is a cornerstone of IDSA’s advocacy efforts. 

IDEA

The International Design Excellence Awards (IDEA) is one of the longest running and most prestigious design awards programs in existence. The competition highlights design in many connected disciplines, including design strategy, branding, digital interaction, and so much more. 

learn more
Student Merit Awards

IDSA recognizes exceptional undergraduate and graduate student design talent through our Student Merit Awards program. The competition highlights the very best creativity, problem solving and design brilliance in each of IDSA’s five North American Districts.

learn more
IDSA Awards

IDSA Awards is a collection of awards presented in recognition of significant contribution to the Society or the industrial design profession, excellence in academic advancement, or personal achievement. 

learn more
20/2X Recognitions

Started in 2020, IDSA Staff recognizes 20 IDSA members who have dedicated their time and talents to the betterment of the Society and the profession as a whole. 

learn more
Member Spotlights

Have you recently won an ID award, launched a new ID program or initiative, written a book on ID, finished a cool project, started or appeared on a design podcast, written an article or been featured in an article about industrial design? Share your ID-related news with IDSA for a chance to be featured in a Member Spotlight on IDSA’s social media channels, website, and/or designBytes e-newsletter! We want to celebrate you, your community, and your great work. 

learn more

关于.text:

INDUSTRIAL DESIGNERS SOCIETY OF AMERICA
Login
SEARCH
INDUSTRIAL DESIGNERS SOCIETY OF AMERICA
About IDSA

We exist to strengthen the industrial design profession. Our members demonstrate the boundless impact of design within business, culture, and society.

Our Story

Founded in 1965, the Industrial Designers Society of America is one of the oldest and largest industrial design associations in existence. Our roots stretch to the beginning of the profession and our membership, past and present, represents some of the most celebrated industrial designers of all time. 

learn more
Advocacy

As a unified body representing the field of industrial design, we work to protect the interests of our membership, the organization itself, and the professional practice of industrial design at large.

learn more
Diveristy, Equity, and Inclusion

Our value is strengthened by elevating voices from divergent backgrounds. IDSA endeavors to confront the historic lack of DEI in industrial design with meaningful action.

learn more
Governance

IDSA is primarily led by passionate volunteers who are governed through a robust system that includes the Board of Directors, committees, councils, Bylaws, policies and procedures.

learn more
Staff

We employ a top-notch, cross-functional, and diverse staff. This talented group of professionals work tirelessly alongside our volunteer leaders, day-in and day-out, to create valuable experiences for our members and community.

learn more

参赛.text:
NEED HELP?
Welcome to the 2026 IDEA


Email notifications for all Round 1 entrants will go out by 5 pm ET on April 17, 2026.

Round 2 entry period: April 17 through May 4, 2026.

The International Design Excellence Awards (IDEA) is an annual competition presented by the Industrial Designers Society of America (IDSA) that recognizes excellence in design across various disciplines. For more than 45 years, IDEA has attracted thousands of exciting and innovative entries from professional designers and students from across the globe, making it one of the most prestigious awards in the industry.

Winning an IDEA is a career-defining milestone that elevates a designer’s work onto a global stage to be celebrated alongside some of the most renowned products in history. 

New This Year: Impact Award
Impact Award asks an essential question: Why does this design matter?

This new, optional award will recognize the entry with the highest score based on the impact statement submitted by the entrant. If you choose "YES" to submit for this award, it will be judged separately from the general entry criteria and will not affect overall results in your primary IDEA entry submission.

An additional entry fee of $100 applies for submissions for this award. For more details, please visit the FAQ section.

Impact Award entrants will be notified of their results in June.

 

Award Categories
Automotive & Transportation
Branding
Children’s Products
Commercial & Industrial Products
Concepts & Speculative Design (NEW)
Consumer Technology
Design Strategy
	
Digital Interaction
Environments
Home
Furniture & Lighting
Lifestyle & Accessories
Medical & Health
Office & Accessories
	
Outdoor & Gardens
Packaging
Service Design
Social Impact Design
Sports & Recreation
Student Projects

 

 

ROUND 1 DEADLINES
	
HOW TO ENTER



Regular Entry:
January 5, 2026– February 13, 2026 (11:59 pm EST)

Late Entry: 
February 14, 2025 – March 11, 2026 (11:59 pm EST)

Entries selected by our judges to advance to Round 2 as finalists will be notified by approximately during the week of April 13, 2026. A separate entry fee will apply to continue in the competition.
See complete Pricing & Deadlines details here.

	

Participation in IDEA 2026 is open to professional and student designers worldwide. In order to be eligible, entries must have been placed into distribution / completed between March 1, 2024 and March 1, 2026. See complete Rules & Regulations here.

Gather the entry materials listed below and submit your entry before March 11, 2026.

IDSA members receive exclusive discounted entry rates for IDEA 2026. Not a member? Click here to join.

 
Information You Will Need To Complete Your Entry

ROUND 1

Written statements describing:
An overview of what the entry is - including key features, functionality, or other details that make this entry a unique product on the market. (150 words or less)
Design innovation - detailing how the entry is a new or novel product, service, or experience. What critical problem does it solve and what is the solution it presents? (250 words or less)
Benefit to user - detailing ways that the end-user benefits from using this design. If applicable, include how this design helps the user accomplish new tasks or otherwise improves their daily life. (250 words or less)
Benefit to client/brand - detailing the business impact of this design. If applicable, include how leveraging design has proven to be a key market differentiator from a business perspective. (250 words or less)
Benefit to society - detailing  how this entry considers social, cultural, and environmental factors. If applicable, include details of this design that apply to its impact on the world. (250 words or less)
Appropriate aesthetics - describe how the final form of the design relates and responds to its use and/or function. If applicable, include how colors, materials, and finishes are effectively used or how aspects of manufacturing influenced its form. (250 words or less)
List of primary materials used in the manufacturing of the entry
Retail price and physical dimensions of the entry
Up to 5 high resolution images of the entry
Optional - a video showcasing the entry features, functionality, and design

ROUND 2

Name of designer(s) and company / design firm who created the entry
Website and email address for promotional materials

 

 

For complete program details, visit IDSA.org/IDEA
